summary: Fixed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES round 1 for M6 succession completion. Regency outcomes now semantically validate that regentCharacterId references an existing, alive, non-incapacitated M3 character, including malformed save rejection. One-candidate low-support pending crises now reject deterministically before commit instead of generating a disputed outcome with the same leading and rival character.

tests_run.result: Red regression run before implementation failed as intended: regency regent semantic invariant was absent after restamping hash, and one-candidate low-support resolve returned invariant-violation from commit instead of structured pre-commit rejection.
Focused R2 regressions passed: 1 file passed; 2 tests passed, 15 skipped.
Focused protocol/succession tests passed after final formatting: 2 files passed, 27 tests passed.
24 files passed, 186 tests passed. Node emitted the existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, M4 232f62ab, M5 bf042e42.
Architecture boundaries passed; sim determinism passed; root Vitest 37 files / 287 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file / 4 tests passed.
Initial post-fix run failed only on Prettier formatting for tests/sim-core-m3-succession-state-machine.test.ts.
Formatted the touched succession test file.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

On feat/m6-succession-completion with six modified allowed-path files: packages/protocol/src/index.ts; packages/save-format/src/index.ts; packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts; packages/sim-core/src/world-state-v0.ts; tests/protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts; tests/sim-core-m3-succession-state-machine.test.ts.

decisions: Regency semantic validation uses the existing M3 available-character definition: a regent must exist in M3 characters and be alive and not incapacitated. Missing regents report bad-reference; unavailable regents report invalid-schema.

Malformed save coverage rebuilds the save envelope checksum and restamps the mutated world hash so the regression reaches semantic-invariant validation instead of being stopped by checksum or hash mismatch.
Low-support one-candidate succession now returns succession-state-invalid at resolve time when no rival exists for a disputed succession, leaving the pending crisis and world hash unchanged.
No UI, content, sim-ai, docs gate, scheduler, RNG, or dependency changes were made in R2.
risks: R4 task with R3-adjacent protocol/save/authoritative-state surface from the existing implementation diff; systems_architect should re-review schema compatibility, save semantic validation, and deterministic command behavior.
The one-candidate low-support path is intentionally a structured rejection rather than an automatic peaceful/regency fallback, preserving the existing support threshold semantics.
requested_action: Review R2 fixes for the two requested succession semantic gaps, then accept or provide further change requests.
